title: MatrixClient: HTTP client for Matrix C-S API (send message)
description:
## What
Implement MatrixClient in bridge-matrix/src/matrix.rs for sending messages to Matrix rooms and parsing incoming AS events.

## Algorithm
1. struct MatrixClient { homeserver: String, as_token: String, http: reqwest::Client }
2. impl MatrixClient::new(homeserver, as_token) -> Self
3. async fn send_text(room_id, text) -> Result<()>: POST /_matrix/client/v3/rooms/{room_id}/send/m.room.message/{txn_id} with body {msgtype:'m.text', body:text} and ?access_token={as_token} auth.
4. Generate txn_id using uuid::Uuid::new_v4().
5. struct MatrixEvent { event_type: String (field: 'type'), room_id: String, sender: String, content: serde_json::Value, event_id: String }
6. struct AsTransaction { events: Vec<MatrixEvent> } — deserialized from the AS PUT body.
7. fn is_text_message(event: &MatrixEvent) -> Option<String>: returns Some(text) if event.type=='m.room.message' and event.content['msgtype']=='m.text', None otherwise.

## Defensive programming
- Never log as_token (only in Authorization header, never in log statements)
- reqwest errors wrapped with context: map_err(|e| anyhow!('Matrix send failed: {e}'))
- txn_id prevents message deduplication on Matrix homeserver

## Tests
- is_text_message_accepts_mtext: create MatrixEvent with type=m.room.message, msgtype=m.text, assert returns Some
- is_text_message_rejects_other_types: msgtype=m.image, assert returns None
- as_transaction_deserializes: JSON string '{events:[{type:m.room.message,...}]}', assert parses correctly
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-bridge-matrix passes; matrix type parsing tests pass
